decisions.reasoning: NVDA had grown to 45% of portfolio — dangerously concentrated. While Q1 FY27 earnings on May 20 were a massive beat ($81.6B actual vs $78.8B est, +85% YoY) and Q2 guidance of $91B topped consensus $87.2B, the stock is 10% off its May 14 ATH ($236.54) and now trading below its 10-day SMA ($220). Bear case is credible: hyperscaler custom silicon (Google TPU v5, Amazon Trainium 2, Microsoft Maia) is shipping production volume, China data center revenue is now permanently baselined at zero, and Rubin architecture delay creates near-term uncertainty. Trimmed 5 shares (21→16) to reduce weight from 45% to ~34%, maintaining meaningful upside while managing concentration risk.

sources.headline: Nvidia Q1 FY27: Record $81.6B Revenue, $91B Q2 Guidance
NVDA Bear Case: Custom silicon, China TAM zero, Rubin delay
Nvidia 20-Trillion Thesis Intact but 2026 allocation cautious

decisions.invalidation: NVDA breaking below slow SMA ($211.56) on elevated volume, or a major hyperscaler cutting AI capex guidance on next earnings print

decisions.reasoning: Initiated 1-share QQQ position (~7.4% weight) using proceeds from NVDA trim to add diversified Nasdaq-100 exposure. QQQ shows a clear long signal (fast SMA $716.90 > slow SMA $687.86, last close $728.55). Macro backdrop is constructive for tech: AI capex estimated $800B in 2026 per BlackRock, S&P 500 earnings growth running at 28% Q1, and Morgan Stanley mid-2027 target of 8,300 on S&P. QQQ diversifies away single-stock NVDA risk while keeping tech upside. Small initial position; can add if the thesis holds.

decisions.invalidation: QQQ breaks below 30-day SMA ($687.86), or Fed surprises with a rate hike that triggers broad multiple compression

decisions.reasoning: Bought yesterday at $312.46, currently flat at $311.46. Q2 2026 earnings were strong: +17% revenue YoY, iPhone 17 sales +22% to $57B, Services record growth. WWDC 2026 upcoming — Melius Research lifted price target citing AI sizzle potential; BofA target $380. SMA signal: long. Position less than 3 days old — no churn.

Gates Foundation sold remaining MSFT stake but for philanthropic reasons (2045 endowment spend pledge), not a bearish signal. decisions.reasoning: Up 2.67% unrealized. Broad market support from strong earnings growth (28% Q1 blended) and AI capex tailwind. However, 30-year bond yields at 2-decade highs above 5% and Shiller PE ~40.7x signal valuation risk; keeping position as portfolio hedge rather than adding.

2026 Market Outlook: Valuation and Technical Concerns, Shiller PE 40.7x

decisions.invalidation: 30-year yield spike above 5.5% or Middle East conflict broadens to major oil supply disruption causing stagflation

summary: First journal entry: trimmed NVDA 21→16 to cut concentration from 45% to 34%, initiated 1-share QQQ for diversified tech exposure; held AAPL, MSFT, SPY. Day P/L +$56.63 (+0.58%).
open_theses: NVDA: Strong AI datacenter fundamentals (85% revenue growth, $91B Q2 guide) but watching custom silicon competition and Rubin delay; slow SMA $211.56 is key support level
AAPL: WWDC 2026 upcoming — AI feature announcements could be major catalyst; BofA $380 target implies 22% upside
QQQ: New position for diversified Nasdaq-100 exposure; add on dips if macro holds
Watching: Bond yields (30yr above 5%) and Fed rate decision as primary macro risk to portfolio multiples
